title: Mass flow rate
body:
In physics and engineering, mass flow rate is the rate at which mass of a substance changes over time.  Its unit is kilogram per second (kg/s) in SI units, and slug per second or pound per second in US customary units.  The common symbol is 
  
    
      
        
          
            
              m
              ˙
            
          
        
      
    
    {\displaystyle {\dot {m}}}
  
 (pronounced "m-dot"), although sometimes 
  
    
      
        μ
      
    
    {\displaystyle \mu }
  
 (Greek lowercase mu) is used.
Sometimes, mass flow rate as defined here is termed "mass flux" or "mass current".
Confusingly, "mass flow" is also a term for mass flux, the rate of mass flow per unit of area.
